title: CCC: full migration from Node.js to Rust — no JS services remaining
description: jkh directive: no Node.js services after this migration. Everything becomes Rust (server) or WASM (client), with a clean client/server boundary. The Rust foundation already exists — dashboard-server (Axum), squirrelchat-server (Axum/SQLite), dashboard-ui (Leptos/WASM). The migration is: retire.ccc/api/index.mjs, replace with a Rust API server that has feature parity, then delete all Node.js service files.

rust_already_exists:
,
      .ccc/dashboard/squirrelchat-server/ — Axum + SQLite, WebSocket, full chat server
node_services_to_retire: squirrelchat/server.mjs — superseded by squirrelchat-server Rust crate

,
      .ccc/scout/github.mjs (308 lines) — GitHub integration, folds into Rust API
squirrelbus/receive-server.mjs — bus receiver, folds into Rust API
ccc-hub/ — entire directory is a mirror of.ccc/api/ — DELETE
node_scripts_to_evaluate: workqueue/scripts/*.mjs — ~30 scripts, evaluate each: keep as one-shot CLI scripts (OK), retire if functionality moves into Rust API, or rewrite as Rust CLI tools

ccc-server (new Rust/Axum binary).storage: SQLite (same pattern as squirrelchat-server — already proven)
ccc-server (new Rust/Axum binary).brain: async background task in same process (tokio spawn), not a separate service
ccc-server (new Rust/Axum binary).scout: async background task (GitHub polling on interval)
ccc-server (new Rust/Axum binary).bus: SQLite-backed message store with SSE/WS broadcast

dashboard-ui (existing Leptos/WASM).change: minor — update API base URLs if needed, otherwise unchanged
dashboard-ui (existing Leptos/WASM).note: Already talks to dashboard-server which proxies to ccc-server

squirrelchat-server (existing Rust).change: retire squirrelchat/server.mjs (Node), squirrelchat-server Rust is already production
squirrelchat-server (existing Rust).note: Auth system (wq-SC-auth-001) should target the Rust server, not the Node one

dashboard-server (existing Rust).change: proxy target changes from Node.ccc/api to new ccc-server binary
dashboard-server (existing Rust).note: Minimal change — update CCC_URL env var

migration_phases.name: Foundation — ccc-server skeleton
work: Implement SQLite schema: agents, heartbeats, queue, projects, secrets, bus_messages
Implement /api/agents/* and /api/heartbeat/* (simplest, most critical for fleet health)
Wire into dashboard-server as CCC_URL target
Run alongside existing Node API (shadow mode — both running, compare outputs)

migration_phases.name: Queue + Brain
work: Implement /api/queue/* with full CRUD + claim/complete/fail lifecycle
Implement brain as tokio background task: poll queue, route by capabilities, claim + dispatch
Feature parity test against existing Node API

migration_phases.name: ClawBus + Scout
work: Implement /bus/* with SQLite backing + SSE broadcast
Implement GitHub scout as background task (replaces.ccc/scout/github.mjs)

migration_phases.name: Auth + User management
work: Port wq-SC-auth-001 auth system to ccc-server (Rust, lettre for email)

migration_phases.name: Cutover + cleanup
work: Run Node API in read-only mode for 24h (smoke test)

tokenhub_audit.note: jkh directive: same audit applies to tokenhub. File as separate task wq-TOKENHUB-audit-001, fire in 1 hour from now (2026-04-01T06:45:00Z). tokenhub has no significant active clients other than CCC fleet — loom never reached production. Potentially the same Rust migration applies.
tokenhub_audit.tokenhub_location: do-host1:8090, source likely in ~/Src or separate repo — Rocky to confirm

risks: workqueue/scripts/*.mjs are openclaw-invoked cron scripts — rewriting them in Rust may not be worth it if openclaw can call a REST endpoint instead
Brain logic is complex (capability routing, stale claim expiry, dedup) — must not regress
SQLite migration: existing data in JSON files needs a one-time import at cutover
open_questions: Should ccc-server be a new crate or extend dashboard-server? (dashboard-server already exists and proxies everything — could merge them)
workqueue/scripts/*.mjs: rewrite in Rust, keep as Node CLI scripts, or replace with API calls?
What is the tokenhub source location? Rocky to confirm.
